title: MongoDB Transactions - Complete Guide (Beginner to Advanced)
description: Comprehensive guide on ACID transactions, multi-document atomicity, and data consistency

transactions_overview.definition: Transactions group multiple operations into atomic, all-or-nothing units ensuring data consistency
transactions_overview.concept: ACID properties: All operations succeed or all fail, no partial updates
purpose: Ensure data consistency across multiple documents
Prevent partial updates if failures occur
acid_properties: Atomicity: All or nothing (entire transaction succeeds or fails)
Consistency: Data stays in valid state
transactions_overview.when_to_use: Multiple operations needing atomic consistency (transfers, enrollments, financial)

definitions.definition: Group of operations executed as atomic unit
definitions.behavior: All succeed or all fail, no partial updates

definitions.example: Transfer $1000: debit one account AND credit other, or neither

definitions.definition: Undo all changes if transaction fails
definitions.example: If commit fails, all changes reversed

section_2_beginner_basic_transaction.title: BEGINNER: Basic Transactions
section_2_beginner_basic_transaction.syntax: Start session → Start transaction → Operations → Commit/Abort

beginner_examples.title: Transfer scholarship between students
beginner_examples.scenario: Move $5000 from Alice's scholarship to Bob's
beginner_examples.code:
session = db.getMongo().startSession()
session.startTransaction()
db.students.updateOne({name: 'Alice'}, {$inc: {scholarship: -5000}}, {session})
db.students.updateOne({name: 'Bob'}, {$inc: {scholarship: 5000}}, {session})
session.commitTransaction()

beginner_examples.title: Simple ACID guarantee
beginner_examples.query: Transaction ensures: Debt removed AND credit added, never just one

section_3_beginner_session.title: BEGINNER: Sessions and Basic Syntax
section_3_beginner_session.definition: Session is context for executing transactions

beginner_examples.title: Abort transaction
beginner_examples.behavior: Rolls back all changes, ends transaction

beginner_examples.title: Try-catch with transaction
beginner_examples.behavior: Abort on error, clean up session

beginner_examples.title: Check transaction success

levels.isolation: Read own writes, others' committed data
levels.concurrent: Doesn't see uncommitted from other transactions

section_6_intermediate_multi_document.title: INTERMEDIATE: Multi-Document Transactions
section_6_intermediate_multi_document.definition: Transactions spanning multiple documents and collections

intermediate_examples.title: Two collections, two operations
intermediate_examples.query: Transaction updates students AND enrollments atomically
intermediate_examples.example: Enroll student: add to enrollments collection, update student.enrollmentCount

section_8_advanced_write_conflicts.title: ADVANCED: Write Conflicts & Retries
section_8_advanced_write_conflicts.definition: Handle conflicts when transactions modify same documents

advanced_examples.title: Conflict detection
advanced_examples.behavior: One succeeds, other gets conflict error

advanced_examples.title: Retry logic

section_9_advanced_deadlock_prevention.title: ADVANCED: Deadlock Prevention
section_9_advanced_deadlock_prevention.definition: Avoid circular locks between concurrent transactions

strategies.description: Always acquire locks in same order
Choose appropriate isolation to prevent conflicts

advanced_examples.title: Cross-shard transaction
advanced_examples.scenario: Update documents on different shards atomically
advanced_examples.example: Transfer funds between customers on different shards

beginner.need: Ensure both debit and credit happen

beginner.example: Create order, deduct inventory, update balance
beginner.need: All or nothing (don't sell if balance insufficient)

beginner.example: Add course, update counts, charge fee

intermediate.example: Decrease inventory, create shipment, update stats

practice_1_keep_short.rule: Keep transactions as short as possible
practice_1_keep_short.good: Only operations requiring atomicity in transaction
practice_1_keep_short.why: Reduces lock time, lowers deadlock probability

practice_2_error_handling.rule: Always handle errors with proper rollback

practice_3_retry_logic.rule: Implement retry logic for transient errors

practice_4_consistent_ordering.rule: Always operate on documents in same order

practice_5_write_concern_majority.rule: Use w: majority for important transactions

mistake_1_nested_transactions.solution: Use single transaction for all operations

mistake_3_no_retry.problem: Fails even though retry would succeed

mistake_4_long_transactions.mistake: Too many operations in one transaction

mistake_5_wrong_isolation.mistake: Use wrong isolation level for use case

mistake_6_no_session_cleanup.solution: Always call endSession in finally block

section_14_performance_implications.title: Performance Considerations
section_14_performance_implications.overhead: Transactions have overhead compared to single operations

summary.core_concept: Transactions ensure atomic, consistent operations across multiple documents
summary.acid_guarantee: All operations succeed or all fail, data stays consistent
summary.best_practice: Keep short, implement retry logic, handle errors properly
summary.progression: Beginner: Basic transactions → Intermediate: Multi-document, isolation → Advanced: Conflicts, distributed
